rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VINCENT GAGLIARDO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Attorney's fee request includes time to address Examiner's Notes (~$2,800) regarding the 5th accounting petition. 2) Petitioner's request for fees as conservator over the person are not sufficiently detailed, including for visits, which lasted a minimum of 3 hours. 3) The fee base for Petitioner's request for guideline fees as conservator over the estate includes an increase in the fair market value of real property, based upon estimates by Realtor.com. However, though the account ends June 18, 2019, a May 2019 valuation is used. It is also noted that Petitioner initially alleged that the fair market value was obtained by averaging Zillow, Trulia, Redfin, and Realtor valuations. 4) Petitioner requests instructions regarding a subpoena for bank records. 5) Court to determine whether all other requests should be granted.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Petitioner Jennifer Shuk-Han Kwok To Furnish Security As Vexatious Litigant
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondent Jeanne Kwong's Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED. The court takes judicial notice of the thirty documents that are pleadings, orders, judgments and oppositions on file with this court, as well as the December 23, 2013 reporter's transcript. (See Evid. Code, §452, subd. (d).) Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. Respondent's Motion for Order Requiring Petitioner Jennifer Shuk-Han Kwok to Furnish Security as Vexatious Litigant is DENIED. Respondent has not shown that Petitioner falls within one of the definitions of a "vexatious litigant" under CCP § 391. Without having made such a determination, the court cannot order Petitioner to furnish a security under CCP § 391.1. In any event, on this showing the court would not order Petitioner to furnish the requested security under CCP § 391.1 Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on Tuesday, December 3, 2019.
